nameMeanings.name: Chamberlin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: occupational name for an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, from Old French chamberlenc ‘chamberlain’. See also Chamberlain and compare above.

variants.name: Chamberlain
meanings: English: status name from Old French chambrelain, Norman French cambrelanc, cambrelen(c) ‘chamberlain’ (of ancient Germanic origin, from kamer ‘chamber, room’, Latin camera (see Chambers ) + the diminutive suffix -(l)ing). This was originally the name of an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, but is so widespread in late medieval England that it must sometimes have been used of people of more ordinary status, perhaps as a nickname for an officious or self-important person or for someone who played the role of chamberlain in a folk play, tableau, or ceremony. Compare Chancellor for a possible similar usage.

variants.name: Chamberland
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Camille, Marcel, Normand, Adrien, Andre, Antoine, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Ghislaine.
French: variant of   Chamberlin  , a cognate of English   Chamberlain  .
History: Simon Chamberland from Chantonnay in Vendée, France, married Marie Boileau in Sainte-Famille, Île d'Orléans, QC, in 1669.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

en.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlin was born in 1485, in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 6 sons and 1 daughter with Sir Edward Hurst. She died in 1545, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60, and was buried in Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australia.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlin est née en 1485, à Oxford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille avec Sir Edward Hurst. Elle est décédée en 1545, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australie.

de.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlin wurde 1485 in Oxford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Edward Hurst mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1545 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlin è nata il nel 1485 nel Oxford, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia con Sir Edward Hurst. Morì nel 1545, nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Unknown Island, Western Australia, Australia.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlinは1485年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアオクスフォードで生まれた。彼女はSir Edward Hurstとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1545年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで60歳で死去し，オーストラリアウエステルン アウストラリアウンクノウン イスランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlin(은)는 1485년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스퍼드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Edward Hurst(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1545년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 오스트레일리아 웨스터 오스트라리야 노운 이슬랜드에 묻혔다.

Ela faleceu em 1545, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Unknown Island, Western Australia, Austrália.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice Francis Chamberlin родилась в 1485 г. в местности оксфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь от Sir Edward Hurst. Она умерла в 1545 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности унноун исланд, вестирн аустраля, Австралия.
